reviews.review_description: Fish Battles is a fun multiplayer combat with health and ammo to pick up as you frag others to get the highest score.  Take out as any as you can as people respawn. Manage your ammo and health to pick up those items as you continue the battle.

Fish Battles is a surprisingly fun and chaotic multiplayer experience where you choose your fish — from sharks to seals to clownfish — each equipped with both primary and secondary weapons. The goal is simple: outscore your opponents, level up, and dominate the ocean!

The controls are intuitive, making it easy to focus on fast-paced combat and strategy. With a solid variety of maps and smooth gameplay, it’s the kind of game you can jump into for a quick match or enjoy for hours with friends.

A great pick for anyone who loves lighthearted competition and wants to meet new players in an underwater world full of action and laughter.
Underwater movement feels completely different from typical VR shooters. Steering has weight and momentum, so it takes some time to learn, but it makes battles feel fresh and immersive.
Swimming and fighting as ocean species instead of humans makes this feel like a brand-new VR experience. Movement isn’t ground-based, and once you adapt, combat becomes really satisfying.

I wasn’t expecting to be controlling a shark or turtle in VR, but it actually works. Swimming around underwater feels weird at first, totally different from normal VR movement, but once you get the hang of it, it’s really fun. The battles are chaotic, the maps are interesting, and seeing all the different creatures just adds to the craziness. Definitely a fresh experience that actually makes VR feel like a new kind of game.
This game is pure underwater chaos. Sea creatures blasting each other in submerged cities is hilarious, fast-paced, and way more fun than I expected in VR.
Explosions, sea creatures, and nonstop battles everywhere. Fish Battles feels like a playful, over-the-top VR battle royale that’s easy to jump into and hard to put down.
